message:
fix(wasm): tick drift, search perf, WASM tick scheduling, JSONL logging

- frankenterm-web/wasm.rs: skip search refresh when query is empty
- ftui-web/step_program.rs: fix tick drift on high-refresh displays by
  snapping last_tick to nearest boundary (preserves remainder)
- ftui-demo-showcase/app.rs: WASM-specific tick scheduling via Cmd::Tick
  since StepProgram does not evaluate subscriptions()
- frankentui_showcase_demo.html: disable JSONL logging by default (expensive
  at 60fps), enable via ?jsonl=1 URL param

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2dui","title":"Coverage Audit & Gap Map (No Mocks)","description":"Purpose: Establish real coverage state (no mocks) across the workspace. Produce a gap map by crate/module/function, highlighting critical logic (render diff/presenter, terminal writer, fairness guard, text width/wrap, widget search/scoring).

Work: Run cargo llvm-cov overall and per crate; capture uncovered lines and paths. Identify any tests that rely on mocks/fakes and propose real-fixture replacements. Flag high-risk areas where logic is complex or user-facing.

Deliverables: Coverage report + prioritized gap list (by file and risk) + recommended test targets per crate beyond CI minimums.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Coverage data collected for workspace + per-crate.
2) Gap list created with file-level targets and risk ranking.
3) Any mock/fake usage called out with proposed real-fixture alternatives.
4) Results recorded in bead notes or a short doc artifact.","notes":"Coverage audit (bd-2dui) — 2026-02-04

Commands attempted:
1) cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --json --summary-only --output-path target/llvm-cov/summary.json
   - Hung in ftui-demo-showcase tests; terminated test binary after ~6m.
   - Observed failures before termination: macro_recorder::tests::error_state_uses_distinct_style_from_recording; markdown_rich_text::tests::{markdown_renders_code_block, markdown_renders_headings, markdown_renders_task_lists}.
2) cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --exclude ftui-demo-showcase --json --summary-only --output-path target/llvm-cov/summary.json
   - Failed on perf-budget tests (no report).
3) cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --exclude ftui-demo-showcase --json --summary-only --output-path target/llvm-cov/summary.json --ignore-run-fail
   - Succeeded; report saved to target/llvm-cov/summary.json.

Overall coverage (excluding ftui-demo-showcase; tests failed but report produced):
- Lines: 56.45% (87,695 / 155,361)
- Functions: 66.16% (10,642 / 16,085)
- Regions: 58.83% (150,579 / 255,957)

Per-crate line coverage (from summary.json):
- ftui: 0.00% (0/8)
- ftui-demo-showcase: 0.00% (0/43,080) — excluded due to hang
- ftui-extras: 3.84% (487/12,668)
- ftui-harness: 67.89% (3,901/5,746)
- ftui-pty: 81.79% (2,457/3,004)
- ftui-runtime: 84.34% (16,293/19,318)
- ftui-widgets: 87.53% (27,819/31,781)
- ftui-render: 90.98% (12,950/14,234)
- ftui-text: 91.56% (7,173/7,834)
- ftui-core: 91.94% (9,569/10,408)
- ftui-i18n: 93.39% (593/635)
- ftui-layout: 96.72% (4,160/4,301)
- ftui-style: 97.82% (2,293/2,344)

Worst-covered files (>=50 lines, 0% lines covered):
- crates/ftui-extras/src/filesize.rs (0/67)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/sampling.rs (0/70)
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/voi_debug_overlay.rs (0/120)
- crates/ftui-pty/src/bin/pty_canonicalize.rs (0/124)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/timer.rs (0/129)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/live.rs (0/130)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/logging.rs (0/155)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs (0/171)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/voi_overlay.rs (0/178)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mod.rs (0/200)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs (0/214)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/image.rs (0/220)
- crates/ftui-text/src/bidi.rs (0/228)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram.rs (0/245)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/filepicker.rs (0/246)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/data.rs (0/264)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/tour.rs (0/267)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/performance.rs (0/274)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/metaballs.rs (0/280)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/notifications.rs (0/281)

Failing tests / budgets encountered during coverage run (need follow-up):
- ftui-render: diff::tests::perf_block_scan_vs_scalar_baseline (crates/ftui-render/src/diff.rs:1522) — 758ms for 1000 iters @200x50 > budget.
- ftui-harness: regression_word_movement_large (crates/ftui-harness/tests/editor_perf.rs:104) — 100 word moves on 10k lines exceeded budget.
- ftui-widgets: focus::spatial::tests::perf_spatial_navigate_100_nodes (crates/ftui-widgets/src/focus/spatial.rs:439) — 400 navigations exceeded budget.
- ftui-widgets: command_palette::scorer perf tests for corpus 100/1000/5000 exceed budgets (crates/ftui-widgets/src/command_palette/scorer.rs:2584, 2605, 2626).
- ftui-widgets: list_panic_on_empty_items_with_selection (crates/ftui-widgets/tests/list_panic.rs:21) — expected None, got Some(0).
- Additional failing targets (details not captured in truncated output):
  - ftui-runtime --lib
  - ftui-runtime --test resize_migration_compat
  - ftui-text --lib
  - ftui-harness --test isomorphism_proofs
  - ftui-harness --test resize_storm_e2e

Mock/fake usage found (needs replacement with real fixtures per policy):
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/subscription.rs: mock subscription for testing.
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/drag.rs: mock drag/drop targets in tests.
- crates/ftui-core/src/caps_probe.rs: MockTerminal for capability tests.

Risk-ranked gaps:
HIGH: ftui-demo-showcase coverage (0%); ftui-extras coverage (3.84%) including visual_fx, logging, filepicker; perf-budget test failures in render/widgets/harness.
MEDIUM: ftui-text bidi.rs (0%); ftui-widgets voi_debug_overlay (0%); ftui-pty bin coverage (0%).
LOW: ftui crate (re-export) 0%.

Next actions suggested:
- Stabilize failing perf budget tests and list_panic regression (blocks reliable coverage runs).
- Add real-fixture tests for ftui-extras and demo-showcase (E2E focus), including visual_fx effects.
- Replace mock-based tests with PTY/harness fixtures (caps_probe, subscription, drag/drop).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:13:29.104150708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:07:05.398834294Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:07:05.398809257Z","close_reason":"Coverage audit complete; notes include gap map, coverage table, failing tests, mock usage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-2enu3","title":"bug(frankenterm-web): harden wasm init + renderer robustness","description":"Fix correctness issues in frankenterm-web wasm bindings + WebGPU renderer:\n\n- init option parsing: reject NaN/non-integers instead of truncating/casting to 0\n- render(): error when renderer not initialized (avoid silent no-op)\n- destroy(): fully reset modifier/IME/composition + encoder state for reuse\n- WebGPU atlas sub-rect uploads: pad bytes_per_row to 256 alignment\n- Surface get_current_texture errors: for Lost/Outdated, reconfigure + retry once","notes":"Implemented wasm init strict numeric parsing + render/destroy guards; fixed WebGPU dirty atlas bytes_per_row 256 alignment and surface Lost/Outdated retry. Fixed failing mermaid JSONL tests (layout_metrics expectation). Pending: workspace cargo test blocked by bd-2yjus program.rs test compile break (MaroonCliff reservation).

New repro from user (mac Chrome + Safari): garbled/noisy grid rendering + slow first paint; console error \"missing required string field: key\" in FrankenTerm.js.

Likely causes/fixes:
1) crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs: GpuRenderer::ensure_glyph_slot still uses rasterize_procedural_glyph for cache misses; wire GlyphCanvasRasterizer::rasterize instead.
2) crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs: parse_key_event currently requires key/code strings via get_string; make tolerant of undefined (use get_string_opt + default), and/or coerce in frankentui_showcase_demo.html with String(e.key ?? \"\") / String(e.code ?? \"\"), and capture keys on window for parity.

---
Update 2026-02-10 (EmeraldForge)
- Root cause for blank/garbled initial render: host HTML did not apply the initial full repaint patch batch produced by ShowcaseRunner.init(); first step can be rendered=false and/or overwrite last_patches.
- Fix: after runner.init(), immediately runner.takeFlatPatches() -> term.applyPatchBatchFlat(...) -> term.render().
- Added cache-busting query params for JS/WASM imports to avoid Cloudflare/browser stale assets.
- Deployed Cloudflare Pages: https://frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/ (preview: https://f3bfb30f.frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/)
- Commit: a47f8215

+{"id":"bd-2enu3","title":"bug(frankenterm-web): harden wasm init + renderer robustness","description":"Fix correctness issues in frankenterm-web wasm bindings + WebGPU renderer:\n\n- init option parsing: reject NaN/non-integers instead of truncating/casting to 0\n- render(): error when renderer not initialized (avoid silent no-op)\n- destroy(): fully reset modifier/IME/composition + encoder state for reuse\n- WebGPU atlas sub-rect uploads: pad bytes_per_row to 256 alignment\n- Surface get_current_texture errors: for Lost/Outdated, reconfigure + retry once","notes":"Implemented wasm init strict numeric parsing + render/destroy guards; fixed WebGPU dirty atlas bytes_per_row 256 alignment and surface Lost/Outdated retry. Fixed failing mermaid JSONL tests (layout_metrics expectation). Pending: workspace cargo test blocked by bd-2yjus program.rs test compile break (MaroonCliff reservation).

New repro from user (mac Chrome + Safari): garbled/noisy grid rendering + slow first paint; console error \"missing required string field: key\" in FrankenTerm.js.

Likely causes/fixes:
1) crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs: GpuRenderer::ensure_glyph_slot still uses rasterize_procedural_glyph for cache misses; wire GlyphCanvasRasterizer::rasterize instead.
2) crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs: parse_key_event currently requires key/code strings via get_string; make tolerant of undefined (use get_string_opt + default), and/or coerce in frankentui_showcase_demo.html with String(e.key ?? \"\") / String(e.code ?? \"\"), and capture keys on window for parity.

---
Update 2026-02-10 (EmeraldForge)
- Root cause for blank/garbled initial render: host HTML did not apply the initial full repaint patch batch produced by ShowcaseRunner.init(); first step can be rendered=false and/or overwrite last_patches.
- Fix: after runner.init(), immediately runner.takeFlatPatches() -> term.applyPatchBatchFlat(...) -> term.render().
- Added cache-busting query params for JS/WASM imports to avoid Cloudflare/browser stale assets.
- Deployed Cloudflare Pages: https://frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/ (preview: https://f3bfb30f.frankentui-showcase-demo.pages.dev/)
- Commit: a47f8215

 {"id":"bd-2f7n6","title":"test(input_parser): add unit tests for ANSI/CSI escape sequence parsing state machine","description":"ftui-core/src/input_parser.rs has ~2,700 lines with zero tests. It's a critical state machine that parses terminal escape sequences into Event objects. Needs tests for: basic printable chars, CSI sequences (cursor move, SGR, etc.), OSC sequences, partial/incomplete sequences, invalid escape codes, modifier key combinations, mouse events, bracketed paste mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltDeer","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:11:16.229976296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:15:47.430154390Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:12:02.376016111Z","close_reason":"Already has comprehensive tests (~98 tests including proptest fuzz), likely added by concurrent agent","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1131,"issue_id":"bd-2f7n6","author":"CobaltDeer","text":"Follow-up from CobaltDeer (post-close): while executing this bead, I added additional parser state-machine coverage in `crates/ftui-core/tests/input_parity_test.rs` without touching `src/input_parser.rs`.

Added tests:
- `parser_partial_csi_buffers_until_final_byte`
- `parser_partial_osc52_buffers_until_terminator`
- `parser_invalid_csi_is_ignored_and_recovers`
- `parser_mouse_sgr_sequence_across_chunks`
- `parser_bracketed_paste_sequence_across_chunks`

Validation:
- `cargo test -p ftui-core --test input_parity_test --features crossterm`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check`

Note:
- `ubs --only=rust crates/ftui-core/tests/input_parity_test.rs` reports many pre-existing test-file `panic!/expect()` findings in this long existing test module; no new scanner category beyond that baseline.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:15:47Z"}]}

- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Keybinding config surface + defaults

 {"id":"bd-2vr","title":"Create comprehensive E2E test suite","description":"# Create comprehensive E2E test suite

## Background
End-to-end tests verify the complete rendering pipeline from widget construction through terminal output. Must test real terminal interactions, not mocked abstractions.

## Technical Requirements

### E2E Test Infrastructure
- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` master script
- Individual test scripts per feature area
- JSON output for CI parsing
- Verbose mode with full trace output
- Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=skip

### Test Categories

#### 1. Rendering Tests (scripts/e2e/render/)
- `hello_world.sh` - Basic text output
- `colors.sh` - Full color palette rendering
- `unicode.sh` - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
- `styles.sh` - Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough
- `hyperlinks.sh` - OSC 8 hyperlink rendering

#### 2. Layout Tests (scripts/e2e/layout/)
- `flex_horizontal.sh` - Row layout constraints
- `flex_vertical.sh` - Column layout constraints
- `grid.sh` - 2D grid positioning
- `nested.sh` - Complex nested layouts
- `resize.sh` - Terminal resize handling

#### 3. Widget Tests (scripts/e2e/widgets/)
- `block.sh` - Borders, titles, padding
- `paragraph.sh` - Text wrapping, alignment
- `table.sh` - Column sizing, selection
- `list.sh` - Selection, scrolling
- `input.sh` - Text entry, cursor

#### 4. Input Tests (scripts/e2e/input/)
- `keyboard.sh` - Key events, modifiers
- `mouse.sh` - Click, scroll, drag
- `paste.sh` - Bracketed paste mode
- `focus.sh` - Focus in/out events

#### 5. Integration Tests (scripts/e2e/integration/)
- `full_app.sh` - Complete application lifecycle
- `inline_mode.sh` - Scrollback-native rendering
- `alt_screen.sh` - Alternate screen buffer
- `raw_mode.sh` - Raw terminal mode transitions

### Test Script Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../common.sh"

TEST_NAME="render/hello_world"
log_start "${TEST_NAME}"

# Run the test binary
output=$(cargo run --example hello_world 2>&1) || fail "Example failed to run"

# Verify output
assert_contains "${output}" "Hello"
assert_no_errors "${output}"

log_pass "${TEST_NAME}"
```

### Logging Requirements
All test scripts must provide detailed logging:

**Start Phase:**
- Timestamp (ISO 8601)
- Test name and category
- Environment info (TERM, COLORTERM, shell)
- Configuration parameters

**Progress Phase:**
- Major checkpoint announcements
- Timing for each phase
- Resource usage snapshots

**Failure Phase:**
- Full context dump
- Expected vs actual comparison
- Stack trace if available
- Terminal state capture
- Reproduction command

**Success Phase:**
- Duration breakdown
- Resource usage summary
- Bytes emitted count

### common.sh Functions
```bash
log_start() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] START: $1"; }
log_progress() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] PROGRESS: $1"; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] PASS: $1 (${SECONDS}s)"; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] FAIL: $1 - $2" >&2; exit 1; }
fail() { log_fail "${TEST_NAME}" "$1"; }
assert_contains() { [[ "$1" == *"$2"* ]] || fail "Expected to contain: $2"; }
assert_no_errors() { [[ "$1" != *"error"* ]] || fail "Found error in output"; }
capture_terminal_state() { echo "TERM=$TERM COLORTERM=$COLORTERM"; }
```

### CI Integration
- JUnit XML output for test runners
- Parallel execution where safe
- Retry logic for flaky tests (max 2 retries)
- Artifact collection on failure (logs, screenshots, terminal state)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Master `e2e_test.sh` script with `--verbose` and `--json` flags
- [ ] `common.sh` with all helper functions
- [ ] 5+ rendering tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ layout tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ widget tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ input tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 3+ integration tests with detailed logging
- [ ] JSON output mode for CI parsing
- [ ] Verbose trace mode showing all operations
- [ ] CI-friendly exit codes (0/1/2)
- [ ] Test duration < 60s total
- [ ] JUnit XML report generation

+{"id":"bd-2vr05","title":"FrankenTermJS: xterm.js-killer parity + beyond","description":"North Star\n- FrankenTermJS replaces xterm.js as the default web terminal engine + renderer.\n- Goals: better correctness, determinism, and performance (WebGPU-first), with a smaller/cleaner API and no legacy baggage.\n\nWhat This Epic Covers\n- Remote terminal mode: consume VT/ANSI byte streams (like xterm.js) and render correctly in the browser.\n- Local ftui-web mode: continue supporting patch-based rendering from ftui-web/ShowcaseRunner.\n- UX parity: scrollback, selection, copy/paste, search, links, resize, IME, accessibility.\n- Compliance: implement the VT subset we claim (see docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md) with conformance fixtures + differential testing.\n\nGuiding Principles\n- Rust-first: parsing/state machine/selection/scrollback live in Rust/WASM, JS is a thin host shim.\n- Deterministic: identical inputs + time + capability profile => identical output; every nondeterminism is explicit and logged.\n- Bounded memory: no unbounded buffers/queues; explicit flow control for remote mode.\n- WebGPU-first: GPU-accelerated, but with graceful fallback when WebGPU is missing.\n\nKey Existing Artifacts\n- Browser surface API: crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs (FrankenTermWeb).\n- WebGPU renderer: crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs.\n- Terminal engine building blocks: crates/frankenterm-core/* (Parser/Grid/Scrollback/Selection/ReplyEngine).\n- Contracts/specs: docs/spec/frankenterm-websocket-protocol.md, docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md, docs/spec/frankenterm-term-capability-profile.md.\n\nNon-Goals\n- Recreating xterm.js addon/plugin system. We will provide first-party feature modules behind stable API methods and explicit capability flags.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:43.039119814Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:43.039119814Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","parity","wasm","web","xterm"]}
+{"id":"bd-2vr05.1","title":"feature(frankenterm-web): remote VT mode (feed bytes + terminal replies)","description":"Background\n- xterm.js is primarily a VT/ANSI terminal emulator: you stream bytes in and it maintains terminal state (grid, scrollback, modes), including responding to terminal queries.\n- FrankenTermWeb currently has feed(data) as a stub in crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs, so true remote terminal mode is not implemented.\n\nGoal\n- Implement browser-side terminal emulation powered by frankenterm-core: VT/ANSI bytes in, WebGPU patches out, plus upstream reply bytes (DA/DSR/CPR/etc).\n\nDeliverables\n- FrankenTermWeb.feed(bytes) updates terminal state + shadow cells + renderer uploads.\n- Add API to drain terminal reply bytes for attach clients (ex: drainReplyBytes(): Uint8Array[]).\n- Deterministic patch+reply behavior (same bytes/time/profile => same outputs).\n\nReferences\n- docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md\n- docs/spec/frankenterm-term-capability-profile.md\n- docs/spec/frankenterm-websocket-protocol.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:38:23.258313503Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:38:23.258313503Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["remote","vt","wasm","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:38:23.258313503Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w5","title":"Implement view_string() easy mode adapter","description":"# Implement view_string() Easy Mode Adapter

## Background
Plan Section 0.8.2 and 12.3 describe a "Easy mode adapter" for trivial apps and prototyping. This allows developers to return a String from view() while still going through the full kernel pipeline.

## Why This Matters
- Enables quick prototyping without learning full Frame API
- Familiar model for developers coming from Bubbletea
- Keeps kernel discipline (String still goes through Frame)
- Lower barrier to entry for simple apps

## Technical Design

### Model Trait Extension
```rust
/// Optional trait for models that want string-based views
pub trait StringModel: Model {
    /// Return view as string (styled text with markup)
    fn view_string(&self) -> String;
}

/// Blanket implementation: view_string -> Frame
impl<M: StringModel> Model for M {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let text = self.view_string();
        let parsed = Text::from_markup(&text);
        frame.render_widget(parsed, frame.area());
    }
}
```

### Alternative: Wrapper Type
```rust
/// Wrapper that converts StringModel to Model
pub struct StringModelAdapter<M: StringModel> {
    inner: M,
}

impl<M: StringModel> Model for StringModelAdapter<M> {
    type Message = M::Message;
    
    fn update(&mut self, msg: Self::Message) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        self.inner.update(msg)
    }
    
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let text = self.inner.view_string();
        let parsed = Text::from_markup(&text);
        frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(parsed), frame.area());
    }
}
```

### Pipeline Preservation
String -> Text/Segments -> Frame draw -> Diff -> Presenter

This ensures:
- Markup is properly parsed
- Styles are correctly applied
- Width measurement is correct
- Diffing works normally
- All kernel guarantees hold

## Usage Example
```rust
struct Counter { count: i32 }

impl StringModel for Counter {
    fn view_string(&self) -> String {
        format\!("[bold]Count:[/] {}", self.count)
    }
}

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    App::string_model(Counter { count: 0 })
        .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 1 })
        .run()
}
```

## API Options

### Option 1: StringModel trait
```rust
App::string_model(model)
```

### Option 2: view() callback
```rust
App::new(model)
    .view(|state| format\!("Count: {}", state.count))
```

### Option 3: Both
Support both patterns for flexibility.

## Tests Required
- [ ] view_string() produces correct Frame output
- [ ] Markup parsing works correctly
- [ ] Multi-line strings render properly
- [ ] Width measurement correct
- [ ] Style application correct
- [ ] Diffing works as expected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StringModel trait or equivalent
- [ ] Integration with App/Program
- [ ] Proper pipeline (not bypass)
- [ ] Documentation with examples
- [ ] Tests for all paths

 {"id":"bd-2wi","title":"Implement Grid layout for 2D positioning","description":"PHASE 5 - GRID LAYOUT

## Background
Grid layout provides 2D positioning with rows/columns. More powerful than Flex for dashboard-style layouts with multiple regions.

## Technical Requirements
- Grid struct with rows × cols dimensions
- Cell addressing: grid[(row, col)]
- Column/row constraints (reuse Flex constraint types)
- Cell spanning: colspan/rowspan for merged cells
- Gap between cells (row_gap, col_gap)
- Area naming: grid.area('sidebar') returns Rect
- Template syntax for declarative grid definition

## Design Rationale
CSS Grid concepts adapted for TUI. Area naming enables semantic layout references. Cell spanning handles common dashboard patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid struct with constraint-based sizing
- [ ] Row and column constraint solving
- [ ] Cell span support (colspan, rowspan)
- [ ] Named area definition and lookup
- [ ] Gap configuration
- [ ] Template DSL for grid definition
- [ ] Overflow handling for too-small grids
- [ ] Property tests for area calculations

## Performance Budget
- Grid solve for 10×10: < 500µs
- Area lookup: O(1) after solve

 {"id":"bd-2wo","title":"Implement tmux/screen passthrough and mux detection","description":"# Implement tmux/screen Passthrough and Mux Detection

## Background
Plan Section 9.4 and ADR-001 describe multiplexer (tmux/screen/zellij) detection and passthrough handling. This is important for inline mode to work correctly under multiplexers.

## Why This Matters
- tmux/screen intercept escape sequences
- Some features (sync output, OSC 8) need passthrough
- Scroll regions behave differently under muxes
- Detection enables correct fallback strategies

## Technical Design

### Detection
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // Multiplexer detection
        let in_tmux = std::env::var("TMUX").is_ok();
        let in_screen = std::env::var("STY").is_ok();
        let in_zellij = std::env::var("ZELLIJ").is_ok();
        
        // Note: WezTerm/Kitty built-in muxes don't need special handling
        
        Self {
            in_tmux,
            in_screen,
            in_zellij,
            // ...
        }
    }
    
    pub fn in_any_mux(&self) -> bool {
        self.in_tmux || self.in_screen || self.in_zellij
    }
}
```

### tmux Passthrough Wrapper
```rust
/// Wrap sequence for tmux passthrough
/// tmux intercepts escape sequences unless wrapped in DCS passthrough
pub fn tmux_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String {
    if in_tmux() {
        // Double escapes for tmux
        let escaped = sequence.replace('\\x1b', "\\x1b\\x1b");
        format\!("\\x1bPtmux;{}\\x1b\\\\", escaped)
    } else {
        sequence.to_string()
    }
}

// Usage example for OSC 8 hyperlinks under tmux
fn emit_hyperlink(url: &str) -> String {
    let osc = format\!("\\x1b]8;;{}\\x1b\\\\", url);
    tmux_wrap(&osc)
}
```

### Mux-Aware Feature Policies
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    /// Returns whether sync output should be enabled
    pub fn use_sync_output(&self) -> bool {
        // Disable in multiplexers (passthrough unreliable)
        if self.in_any_mux() {
            return false;
        }
        self.sync_output
    }
    
    /// Returns whether scroll-region optimization is safe
    pub fn use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        // Conservative in muxes due to quirks
        if self.in_any_mux() {
            return false;  // Fall back to overlay redraw
        }
        self.scroll_region
    }
    
    /// OSC 8 hyperlink support
    pub fn use_hyperlinks(&self) -> bool {
        // tmux passthrough needed for hyperlinks
        // Only enable if passthrough is reliable
        if self.in_tmux {
            // Modern tmux (3.3+) supports OSC 8 passthrough
            // but detection is fragile
            return false;  // Conservative default
        }
        self.osc8_hyperlinks
    }
}
```

### Screen Passthrough
```rust
/// screen uses different passthrough syntax
pub fn screen_wrap(sequence: &str) -> String {
    if in_screen() {
        // screen uses P escape sequence
        format\!("\\x1bP{}\\x1b\\\\", sequence)
    } else {
        sequence.to_string()
    }
}
```

### Zellij Handling
```rust
// Zellij generally has better passthrough than tmux/screen
// but some features still need care
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn zellij_passthrough_mode(&self) -> bool {
        // Zellij 0.39+ has improved passthrough
        self.in_zellij
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **TerminalCapabilities::detect()** - Detect multiplexer presence
2. **Presenter output** - Apply passthrough wrapping where needed
3. **ADR-001 inline mode** - Fall back to overlay redraw in muxes
4. **Feature policies** - Conservative defaults in mux environments

## Tests Required
- [ ] TMUX env var detection
- [ ] STY env var detection (screen)
- [ ] ZELLIJ env var detection
- [ ] tmux_wrap produces correct output
- [ ] screen_wrap produces correct output
- [ ] Feature policies respect mux detection
- [ ] PTY test under tmux (if CI supports)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mux detection in TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] in_any_mux() helper method
- [ ] Passthrough wrappers for tmux/screen
- [ ] Feature policies mux-aware
- [ ] Documentation of mux limitations

 {"id":"bd-3hy","title":"Define per-module unit test coverage requirements","description":"# Testing: Unit Test Coverage Matrix (per crate/module)

## Purpose
This bead defines **explicit, per-crate unit test expectations** so we can enforce correctness while the codebase grows.

This is intentionally opinionated: the kernel is the product, so kernel crates have higher coverage requirements.

## How To Use
- When adding a new module, add it to the matrix.
- When adding a new public API, add explicit unit tests here.
- CI should enforce these thresholds (see bd-xn2).

---

## Coverage Targets (v1)
These are *minimum* targets. For kernel-critical modules, we should aim higher.

- ftui-render: ≥ 85% (kernel)
- ftui-core: ≥ 80% (terminal/session + input)
- ftui-style: ≥ 80%
- ftui-text: ≥ 80%
- ftui-layout: ≥ 75%
- ftui-runtime: ≥ 75%
- ftui-widgets: ≥ 70%
- ftui-extras: ≥ 60% (feature-gated; correctness still required, but scope is optional)

Note: Some integration-heavy paths (PTY tests) will not show up as unit coverage; they are enforced separately.

---

## ftui-render (≥ 85%)
Kernel correctness lives here.

### Cell / CellContent / CellAttrs
- [ ] CellContent creation from char vs grapheme-id
- [ ] Width semantics (ASCII, wide, combining, emoji)
- [ ] Continuation-cell sentinel semantics for wide glyphs
- [ ] PackedRgba: construction + Porter-Duff alpha blending
- [ ] CellAttrs: bitflags operations + merge/override
- [ ] 16-byte Cell layout invariants (size/alignment) + bits_eq correctness

### Buffer
- [ ] Create/resize buffer with dimensions
- [ ] get/set bounds checking + deterministic defaults
- [ ] Clear semantics (full vs region)
- [ ] Scissor stack push/pop semantics (intersection monotonicity)
- [ ] Opacity stack push/pop semantics (product in [0,1])
- [ ] Wide glyph placement + continuation cells
- [ ] Iteration order and row-major storage assumptions

### Diff
- [ ] empty diff (no changes)
- [ ] single cell change
- [ ] row changes
- [ ] run grouping behavior
- [ ] scratch buffer reuse (no unbounded allocations)

### Presenter
- [ ] cursor tracking correctness
- [ ] style tracking correctness
- [ ] link tracking correctness (OSC 8 open/close)
- [ ] single-write-per-frame behavior
- [ ] synchronized output behavior where supported (fallback correctness)

---

## ftui-core (≥ 80%)

### Event types
- [ ] canonical key/mouse/resize/paste/focus event types are stable

### InputParser
- [ ] bounded CSI/OSC/DCS parsing (DoS limits)
- [ ] bracketed paste decoding + max size
- [ ] mouse SGR decoding
- [ ] focus/resize event decoding

### TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] env heuristic detection (TERM/COLORTERM)
- [ ] mux flags (tmux/screen/zellij) correctness

### TerminalSession lifecycle
- [ ] RAII enter/exit discipline
- [ ] panic cleanup paths are idempotent

---

## ftui-style (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Style defaults + builder ergonomics
- [ ] deterministic style merge (explicit masks)
- [ ] Color downgrade (truecolor→256→16→mono)
- [ ] Theme presets + semantic slots
- [ ] StyleSheet registry + named style composition

---

## ftui-text (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Segment system correctness (Cow<str>)
- [ ] width measurement correctness + LRU cache behavior
- [ ] grapheme segmentation helpers for wrap/truncate correctness
- [ ] wrap/truncate semantics for ZWJ/emoji/combining
- [ ] markup parser correctness (feature-gated)

---

## ftui-layout (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Rect operations (intersection/contains)
- [ ] Flex constraint solving + gaps
- [ ] Grid placement + spanning + named areas
- [ ] Measurement algebra (min/max + clamp)

---

## ftui-runtime (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Program/Model/Cmd lifecycle + scheduler
- [ ] determinism invariants (simulator)
- [ ] subscriptions/ticks integration

---

## ftui-widgets (≥ 70%)
Harness essentials must be tested.

- [ ] Panel/Block borders + title placement
- [ ] Paragraph wrapping + alignment
- [ ] Table sizing + selection
- [ ] Viewport/log viewer scrolling
- [ ] TextInput cursor + edit behavior
- [ ] Progress/spinner rendering

---

## Test Quality Requirements
- Each test is deterministic (no wall-clock timing).
- Each test is fast (target <10ms).
- Failures must be actionable (expected vs actual grid/segments, not “assertion failed”).

## Property Testing
Use property tests for:
- diff correctness
- presenter roundtrip via terminal model
- text width/wrap invariants
- layout solver invariants

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The matrix matches the actual crate boundaries (no stale mapping).
- [ ] CI encodes these thresholds (or a deliberate waiver is documented).
- [ ] Adding a new module requires adding it to this matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:29:14.532567931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816690672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816665214Z","close_reason":"Expanded coverage matrix with per-module expectations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hy","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":428,"issue_id":"bd-3hy","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopening per user request; keep issues open until explicitly agreed to close.","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3i6","title":"Implement bracketed paste and mouse SGR protocol","description":"PHASE 3 - ADVANCED INPUT PROTOCOLS

## Background
Bracketed paste mode prevents pasted content from being interpreted as commands. SGR mouse protocol provides precise mouse reporting with modifiers.

## Technical Requirements

### Bracketed Paste Mode
Escape sequences:
- Enable: ESC[?2004h
- Disable: ESC[?2004l
- Paste start: ESC[200~
- Paste end: ESC[201~

pub struct BracketedPasteParser {
    in_paste: bool,
    paste_buffer: Vec<u8>,
    max_paste_size: usize,  // Default: 1MB
}

impl BracketedPasteParser {
    pub fn parse(&mut self, byte: u8) -> Option<InputEvent> {
        if self.in_paste {
            // Accumulate until paste end sequence
            // Enforce max_paste_size limit
        } else {
            // Check for paste start sequence
        }
    }
}

pub enum InputEvent {
    Key(KeyEvent),
    Mouse(MouseEvent),
    Paste(String),  // Complete paste content
    Resize(u16, u16),
    Focus(bool),
}

### SGR Mouse Protocol (1006)
Escape sequence: ESC[<Cb;Cx;CyM or ESC[<Cb;Cx;Cym

struct SgrMouseParser;

impl SgrMouseParser {
    /// Parse SGR extended mouse report
    pub fn parse(&self, params: &[u8]) -> Option<MouseEvent> {
        // Parse: <button;column;rowM or <button;column;rowm
        // Button encoding:
        //   0 = left, 1 = middle, 2 = right
        //   +4 = shift, +8 = meta, +16 = ctrl
        //   32 = motion, 64 = scroll
        // M = press, m = release
    }
}

pub struct MouseEvent {
    pub kind: MouseEventKind,
    pub column: u16,
    pub row: u16,
    pub modifiers: KeyModifiers,
}

pub enum MouseEventKind {
    Down(MouseButton),
    Up(MouseButton),
    Drag(MouseButton),
    Moved,
    ScrollDown,
    ScrollUp,
    ScrollLeft,
    ScrollRight,
}

### Enable/Disable Mouse
pub fn enable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    // Enable SGR extended mode (1006)
    // Enable any-event tracking (1003) or button-event (1002)
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1000h\x1b[?1006h")?;
    Ok(())
}

pub fn disable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1006l\x1b[?1000l")?;
    Ok(())
}

### Paste Size Limits
- Default max: 1MB
- Configurable via builder
- Large pastes: return error or truncate

### Focus Events (bonus)
- Enable: ESC[?1004h
- Disable: ESC[?1004l
- Focus in: ESC[I
- Focus out: ESC[O

## Tests Required
- [ ] Bracketed paste parse correct
- [ ] Paste size limit enforced
- [ ] SGR mouse button decoding
- [ ] SGR mouse position decoding
- [ ] Modifier key detection
- [ ] Scroll events
- [ ] Motion events
- [ ] Focus events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bracketed paste with size limit
- [ ] SGR mouse protocol parsing
- [ ] All mouse event types
- [ ] Modifier key capture
